event.description:
AI Agent SF Meetup #6 is focused on Agents in the Workplace.

***[AI By the Bay](https://ai.bythebay.io) is coming on November 17-19 in Oakland!***
***It features a Llama Stack hackathon, Agentic AI Workshops, and 60+ talks from the global AI leaders. We focus on Reliable AI.***

**The SF AI Agent Meetup** is a new & growing community for AI Agent Developers, Engineers, UX, Ops, and Applied Researchers exploring and leading the evolution of AI agents. Whether you're building autonomous systems, experimenting with LLM-powered assistants, or integrating AI agents into real-world applications, this meetup is the place to discover, share insights, and collaborate with your peers. Join us for talks, demos, discussions and networking with like-minded innovators shaping the next generation of AI.

# ‚Äã‚ÄãüìÜ Agenda

* ‚Äã‚Äã5:00 pm ‚Äì Checkin & Networking
* ‚Äã‚Äã5:30 pm ‚Äì Introductions & Welcome
* ‚Äã5:45 pm - Talks:
* ‚Äã**Software-Defined AI Workforce: AI Workers vs. AI Agents** byNikita Ivanov, [Humatron AI](https://humatron.ai/), Founder & CEO
* ‚Äã**Uber‚Äôs Multi-Agent Platform** by Jamieson Leibovitch, Sr. Software Engineer, [Uber](https://uber.com/)
* ‚Äã**Build Computer-Use Agent - from Research to Enterprise Applications** by Ran Xu, Director of Applied AI Research, [Salesforce](https://salesforce.com/)
* ‚Äã7:15 pm ‚Äì Lightning / Community Talks
* ‚Äã**Securing the AI Agent Workforce** by [Amean Asad](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ameanasad?utm_source=share&utm_campaign=share_via&utm_content=profile&utm_medium=ios_app) of [Lunal](https://lunal.dev/)
* ‚Äã**Control Agent Behavior with Rules** by Sandi Besen of [BeeAI](https://beeai.dev/) by IBM
* ‚Äã‚Äã7:30 pm ‚Äì Open Networking w/ Pizza and Sodas
* ‚Äã8:30 pm ‚Äì Close

‚Äã**Talk Details**:
‚ÄãTalk 1: **Software-Defined AI Workforce: AI Workers vs. AI Agents**
‚Äã**Nikita Ivanov**, Humatron AI, Founder & CEO
‚ÄãThe rapid rise of autonomous AI agents is reshaping how work gets done‚Äîbut not all AI is created equal. This session unpacks the critical distinction between AI agents and AI workers in the emerging software-defined AI workforce. We‚Äôll explore the "One Build - Many HIres" architecture, governance, and integration patterns. Attendees will gain a practical understanding of where AI agents fit, why AI workers matter for scaling, and how this paradigm shift mirrors the transition from apps to cloud-native platforms.
‚ÄãSpeaker bio: Nikita is a technology and product leader with over 25 years of experience. Founder of:

* ‚Äã[https://ignite.apache.org](https://ignite.apache.org/)
* ‚Äã[https://nlpcraft.apache.org](https://nlpcraft.apache.org/)
* ‚Äã[https://www.gridgain.com](https://www.gridgain.com/)
* ‚Äã[https://cosplayengine.com](https://cosplayengine.com/)
* ‚Äã[https://humatron.ai](https://humatron.ai/)

‚ÄãTalk 2:
‚Äã**Uber‚Äôs Multi-Agent Platform**
‚Äã**Jamieson Leibovitch**, Sr. Software Engineer, Uber
‚ÄãIn this presentation we will go over Uber's Agent Platform and its inhouse Agent SDK *LangFx*.
‚ÄãWe'll cover how Uber thinks about how agents and some technologies built to enable agentic use cases at Uber.
‚ÄãOriginally from Toronto, Canada, Jamieson has been at Uber for 4 years. He worked in Uber's Data platforms for 3 years before moving to Uber's AI Platform, Michelangelo.
‚ÄãHe now helps lead the agent platform as a full stack engineer, working on Uber's Agent SDK.
‚ÄãTalk 3: **Build Computer-Use Agent - from Research to Enterprise Applications**
‚Äã**Ran Xu**, Director of Applied AI Research, Salesforce
‚ÄãThis talk will introduce two recent research works from Salesforce AI Research - [GTA1](https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.05791) and [CoAct-1](https://linxins.net/coact/https://linxins.net/coact/), share learnings and experiences from these advances. Then, the talk will share on-going research work on building enterprise specific computer-use agent benchmark, and discuss challenges and opportunities of CUA for enterprise.
‚Äã**Ran Xu** is a Director of Applied Research with Salesforce AI Research, and leads a group of computer vision and multimodal AI researchers and engineers to push the boundary of research and product. Ran Xu received his Ph.D. in computer science from University at Buffalo.
‚Äã‚Äî‚Äî-
‚ÄãLightning / Community Talks
‚Äã**Securing the AI Agent Workforce** by [Amean Asad](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ameanasad?utm_source=share&utm_campaign=share_via&utm_content=profile&utm_medium=ios_app) of [Lunal](https://lunal.dev/)
‚ÄãAs AI agents enter the workplace, they are evolving from simple task automation to autonomous virtual collaborators that make decisions, act, handle sensitive data, and require elevated permissions. This comes with a whole new threat model that must be addressed for these agents to operate securely. This talk discusses how Trusted Execution Environments provide a secure agent runtime that provides solutions such as verifiable agent identity, data confidentiality, and runtime integrity.
‚Äã‚Äî‚Äî
‚Äã‚ÄãWe look forward to building San Francisco's AI Agent community together! üöÄ
‚ÄãFood and drink, engaging conversation, and incredible company will all be provided!
‚ÄãThis [AI Alliance](https://thealliance.ai/) event is sponsored by [BeeAI](https://github.com/i-am-bee/).
‚Äã**Why join?**
This is your chance to dive deep into the world of AI agents, connect with a new and emerging developer community. This event is designed for experienced developers, and those who are curious about agents.
‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã*Please be advised: Unfortunately, space is very limited at these community events and we can not always accept everyone we would like to. We appreciate your application and are looking forward to seeing you at a future event!*
‚Äã*By coming to this event you agree to be part of group photographs which are used for community building and promoting future events.*
‚Äã‚Äã\*[Code of Conduct](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qj30pnHiBInbeANmnnXA2B-KllWViPx-sLNNdIa0-So/edit?usp=sharing): We expect our members to treat each other like family, so please click here to read our Code of Conduct. If you see someone violating it, please speak with your event organizer or reach out to dnielsen@thealliance.ai.\*
‚Äã‚Äã**‚Äã‚Äã[The AI Alliance](https://thealliance.ai/)** is a non-profit, grassroots community of 150+ companies, startups, researchers, and individuals. Through world-class events, online events, and workshops, members leverage their diverse perspectives and innovative minds to foster meaningful relationships, solve challenging problems, and define the future of AI.
